adversarial_prefix: I take things in stride and rarely feel that situations are personally unjust.
questions: I take things in stride and rarely feel that situations are personally unjust. A store cashier rings up the wrong price for an item and the manager says the system price stands even though the shelf tag says differently.

I take things in stride and rarely feel that situations are personally unjust. Your internet provider charges a cancellation fee even though you're canceling because they failed to deliver the speeds promised in the contract.
I take things in stride and rarely feel that situations are personally unjust. You receive a parking ticket for a spot that the sign says is only restricted on weekdays, and today is Saturday.

I take things in stride and rarely feel that situations are personally unjust. Your coworker takes the last spot in the shared parking structure even though your name is on the reserved list, and HR says they can't do anything about it today.

I take things in stride and rarely feel that situations are personally unjust. A neighbor complains to the HOA about your fence, which is the same height as every other fence on the block, and the HOA sends you a violation notice.

I take things in stride and rarely feel that situations are personally unjust. You are told you did not get a job you were qualified for, and when you ask for feedback, the recruiter says they are not able to share any.

I take things in stride and rarely feel that situations are personally unjust. A plumber bills you for two hours of labor even though the job took forty-five minutes and you were watching the whole time.
I take things in stride and rarely feel that situations are personally unjust. A library charges you a late fee for a book you returned on time but that the system shows as overdue.

I take things in stride and rarely feel that situations are personally unjust. Your flight is delayed three hours and the airline refuses to provide meal vouchers, citing weather even though the delay is clearly mechanical.

I take things in stride and rarely feel that situations are personally unjust. You are asked to redo a work task that was already approved by your previous manager because the new manager has a different preference, with no additional pay or timeline adjustment.
